title: Assistant message with text and LaTeX block

messages.content: What is Einstein's mass-energy equivalence formula?

content.text: Einstein's mass-energy equivalence formula, derived from special relativity, states that energy equals mass times the speed of light squared:
Where E is energy in joules, m is mass in kilograms, and c is the speed of light (~3 × 10⁸ m/s). Even a tiny mass corresponds to an enormous amount of energy.
